msg: No tuning artifacts found for croqtile/matmul_e4m3fp16_8192x8192x4096
PLANNED iter002_launch_bounds: Add launch_bounds(256,2) to force 2 blocks/SM, reduce register pressure from 153 to ~128
PLANNED iter003_swiz_group4: Add block ID swizzle with group_size=4 to improve L2 cache reuse
PLANNED iter004_stages3: Increase pipeline stages from 2 to 3 for better memory latency hiding
PLANNED iter005_wn192: STRUCTURAL: Increase WARP_N from 128 to 192 for better compute/memory ratio
PLANNED iter006_wn256: STRUCTURAL: Increase WARP_N to 256 (8192 divisible by 256) for better compute density
PLANNED iter007_warpspec_1p2c: STRUCTURAL: 1p2c warp specialization with TILE_M=128, better producer/consumer overlap
PLANNED iter008_swiz64: Reduce shared memory bank conflicts by changing swizzle from 128 to 64 bytes - 8-way bank conflicts causing 87.5% overhead on shared stores
PLANNED iter008_stages3: Increase pipeline stages from 2 to 3 with 1p2c warp spec - 39.2% barrier stall indicates insufficient pipeline depth to hide memory latency
PLANNED iter009_tile192: Increase TILE_N from 128 to 192 for better compute-to-memory ratio. Larger tile amortizes bank conflict overhead by doing more compute per shared memory access
PLANNED iter009_tile256: Increase TILE_N/WARP_N from 128 to 256 for better compute-to-memory ratio. Larger tile amortizes bank conflict overhead by doing more compute per shared memory access
PLANNED iter010_stages4: Increase pipeline stages from 3 to 4 with 1p2c warp spec. More stages can further hide memory latency and reduce barrier stalls (39% of stalls were barrier-related)
PLANNED iter011_stages5: Increase pipeline stages from 4 to 5. Continue deepening pipeline to maximize memory latency hiding with the 1p2c structure
PLANNED iter012_warpspec1p3c: Switch to 1p3c warp specialization (1 producer + 3 consumers) with 4 stages. More consumer warpgroups can increase compute parallelism and SM utilization
PLANNED iter012_tilek256: Increase TILE_K from 128 to 256 to reduce K loop iterations by half, thereby reducing barrier synchronization overhead between producer and consumers
PLANNED iter012_swizzle: Add block ID swizzling with group size 4 to iter010 (4-stage 1p2c). L2 hit rate was 57%, swizzling can improve L2 locality for rhs tiles accessed by multiple CTAs

PLANNED iter013_swiz3stg: Reduce pipeline stages from 4 to 3 while keeping block swizzle. This reduces shared memory usage which may allow higher occupancy. With swizzle improving L2 locality, fewer stages may suffice to hide memory latency
PLANNED iter014_swizgrp8: Try larger swizzle group (8 instead of 4) with 4-stage 1p2c. A larger group may better amortize L2 prefetch costs and improve data reuse across more CTAs
PLANNED iter015_swizgrp16: Try even larger swizzle group (16 instead of 8). Continue the trend from group 4->8 improvement
PLANNED iter016_swizgrp32: Try swizzle group 32, continuing the 4->8->16 improvement trend
PLANNED iter017_regbound: Force lower register usage with __launch_bounds__(384, 2) to try to achieve 2 blocks per SM. Current 157 regs/thread limits to 1 block/SM
PLANNED iter018_warpspec1p1c: Try 1p1c (simpler warp spec) with swizzle group 16 and 4 stages. Simpler structure may use fewer registers and allow better occupancy
PLANNED iter019_tilem64: Try TILE_M=64 (equal to WARP_M) with 1p2c structure. This changes each consumer to work on a narrower M slice, potentially reducing shared memory pressure and register usage
PLANNED iter019_tilen64: Try TILE_N=64 with WARP_N=64 for 1p2c to reduce rhs shared memory footprint. Smaller N tile may allow more blocks per SM
PLANNED iter020_swizgrp12: Try swizzle group 12, searching for potential sweet spot between 8 (573 TFLOPS) and 16 (580 TFLOPS)
PLANNED iter021_stages2swiz: Try 2 stages with swizzle group 16. With swizzle improving L2 hit rate (DRAM throughput 10%), fewer stages may suffice while reducing shared memory and register pressure
PLANNED iter022_swizgrp20: Try swizzle group 20, between 16 (580 TFLOPS) and 32 (576 TFLOPS) to check for finer-grained optimum
PLANNED iter023_sasscomp: Perform SASS comparison between cuBLAS baseline and iter015 to identify instruction-level differences and optimization opportunities
Custom SASS extracted for iter015_swizgrp16 (1899 lines)
Baseline SASS extracted via ncu (1186 lines)
SASS comparison complete for iter015_swizgrp16 (saved to tuning/sm90_NVIDIA_H800_PCIe/croqtile/perf/matmul_e4m3fp16_8192x8192x4096/opus-4/sass_compare_iter015_swizgrp16.json)

PLANNED iter024_noppc: Try non-producer-consumer pipelining: all warps participate in both load and compute phases. This avoids the high barrier density (15x vs baseline) inherent to warp specialization
PLANNED iter025_unroll: Add #pragma unroll to WGMMA k-loop to reduce loop overhead. The inner iv_warp loop iterates 4x per k-tile.
PLANNED iter026_prefetch: Add TMA prefetch for next k-tile during WGMMA computation. Prefetch k+1 while computing k.

PLANNED iter026_warpspec1p3c: Try 1p3c warp specialization (1 producer + 3 consumers). Current 1p2c has 384 threads; 1p3c would have 512 threads with 3 warp groups for compute. May improve compute throughput.
PLANNED iter027_swizgrp14: Try swizzle group 14 - finer search between group 12 (574.99) and group 16 (580.02).
PLANNED iter028_swizgrp18: Try swizzle group 18 - finer search between group 16 (580.02) and group 20 (574.62).
PLANNED iter029_fastmath: Add --use_fast_math nvcc flag to enable fast math intrinsics. May improve FP32 scale operations.
PLANNED iter030_maxreg120: Try -maxrregcount=120 to force register spilling. May increase occupancy at cost of spill overhead.
PLANNED iter031_maxreg110: Try -maxrregcount=110 - more aggressive register limit than 120. May increase occupancy further.
PLANNED iter032_maxreg130: Try -maxrregcount=130 - between 120 (best) and default ~157. Binary search.
PLANNED iter033_maxreg115: Try -maxrregcount=115 - between 110 (too aggressive) and 120 (best).
PLANNED iter034_maxreg120fast: Combine -maxrregcount=120 with --use_fast_math. May have synergy.
PLANNED iter035_maxreg118: Try -maxrregcount=118 - between 115 (failed) and 120 (best). Find exact boundary.
PLANNED iter036_maxreg119: Try -maxrregcount=119 - final binary search between 118 (failed) and 120 (best).
PLANNED iter037_stages5maxreg: Combine 5-stage pipeline with -maxrregcount=120. Previously 5 stages was worse, but may work better with register limit.
PLANNED iter038_dlcmca: Add -Xptxas -dlcm=ca (cache all loads to L1) with maxreg=120. May improve memory latency.
PLANNED iter039_swiz24maxreg: Try swizzle group 24 with maxreg=120. Unexplored sweet spot between 20 and 32?
PLANNED iter040_swizzled_output: Fix 8-way bank conflict in shared memory output store by using swizzled SMEM layout for output buffer (XOR-based swizzle)
PLANNED iter040_pingpong: Implement ping-pong consumer scheduling - consumer0 works on even stages while consumer1 works on odd stages to reduce barrier contention
PLANNED iter040_stages3_maxreg: Reduce pipeline stages from 4 to 3 with maxreg=120 to reduce barrier overhead while maintaining latency hiding
PLANNED iter041_scale_prefetch: Prefetch scale factors to shared memory at K-block boundaries to improve coalescing
PLANNED iter042_tilem64: Reduce TILE_M from 128 to 64 (single consumer instead of 2) to halve register footprint and double occupancy
PLANNED iter042_wg_arrive_once: Move warpgroup_arrive() outside inner warp loop - call once per K iteration instead of 4 times
PLANNED iter043_tilek256: Double K-tile from 128 to 256 to increase arithmetic intensity per barrier sync (8 WGMMA ops instead of 4)
PLANNED iter043_ldg_prefetch: Use __prefetch_l2 for scale factors to hide global memory latency
Custom SASS extracted for iter030_maxreg120 (1933 lines)
SASS comparison complete for iter030_maxreg120 (saved to tuning/sm90_NVIDIA_H800_PCIe/croqtile/perf/matmul_e4m3fp16_8192x8192x4096/opus-4/sass_compare_iter030_maxreg120.json)
PLANNED iter044_arrive_outside: Move warpgroup_arrive() outside inner warp loop to reduce barrier overhead (42.6% stall -> expected 30%)
PLANNED iter045_warpn64_1p2c: WARP_N=64 with 1p2c: reduce accumulator registers per consumer, potentially higher occupancy
PLANNED iter045_wait_before_scale: Move warpgroup_wait<0>() BEFORE scale_accumulator - WGMMA must complete before reading accumulators
PLANNED iter046_tilem256_1p4c: TILE_M=256 with 1p4c (4 consumers each handling 64 rows) - more compute per CTA, potentially better occupancy
